title: Reverse and Rotate a std::array
prompt_md:
## Goal
Read integers into a `std::array`, reverse it in-place using a function template, then rotate the reversed array left by one position. Print the array after each operation.

## Input
- A single line containing exactly 6 space-separated integers.

## Output
- Line 1: the array after reversing, elements separated by spaces.
- Line 2: the array after rotating the reversed array left by one (the first element moves to the end), elements separated by spaces.

No trailing spaces on either line.

## Requirements
1. Declare a `std::array<int, 6>` and read all 6 values from stdin.
2. Write a function template:
   ```
   template <typename T, std::size_t N>
   void reverseArray(std::array<T, N>& arr)
   ```
   that reverses the array in-place by swapping elements from both ends toward the middle. Use `arr.size()` to determine bounds.
3. Write a function template:
   ```
   template <typename T, std::size_t N>
   void rotateLeft(std::array<T, N>& arr)
   ```
   that shifts every element one position to the left and places the original first element at the end. Use a loop with `operator[]`.
4. Write a function template:
   ```
   template <typename T, std::size_t N>
   void printArray(const std::array<T, N>& arr)
   ```
   that prints all elements separated by single spaces (no trailing space) followed by `'\n'`.
5. In `main`, read the array, call `reverseArray`, print, call `rotateLeft`, print.
6. Do not use any standard library algorithm (no `<algorithm>`). Do not use `std::vector`.

title: Copy Between C-style Array and std::array
prompt_md:
## Goal
Read integers into a C-style array, copy them into a `std::array` using pointer arithmetic, compute the sum using the `std::array`, and print both the copied array and the sum.

## Input
- A single line containing exactly 5 space-separated integers.

## Output
- Line 1: `Array:` followed by a space, then the 5 elements separated by spaces (no trailing space).
- Line 2: `Sum:` followed by a space, then the sum of all elements.

## Requirements
1. Declare a C-style array `int cArr[5]{};` and read 5 integers into it using index notation.
2. Declare a `std::array<int, 5> sArr{};`.
3. Copy from `cArr` to `sArr` using a pointer-based loop: declare `const int* ptr{ cArr };` and iterate while `ptr < cArr + 5`, using pointer arithmetic to compute the index (`static_cast<std::size_t>(ptr - cArr)`) for `sArr`.
4. Write a function template:
   ```
   template <typename T, std::size_t N>
   T computeSum(const std::array<T, N>& arr)
   ```
   that returns the sum of all elements using a range-based `for` loop.
5. In `main`, call `computeSum` on `sArr` and print the output in the format above.
6. Do not use `std::copy`, `<algorithm>`, `std::vector`, or `memcpy`.
